contenders.handicapper_notes: Highly impressive debut runner-up on June 26 over this track and trip, showing excellent early speed to lead before getting tired late. Drawn perfectly on the rail to secure the early lead, which is a massive advantage over the 1000m sprint course at Busan. Expect him to clear this field early and prove very hard to catch.

Debut colt by Old Fashioned who showed moderate ability in his June 25 trial, finishing 8th. He gets a favorable low draw to save ground, but will need to show significant improvement from his trial performance to threaten the top selection.
First-starter by Race Day who finished 7th in her June 25 trial. She has an inside draw which helps her tactical positioning, but trial times suggest she may find a few of these too sharp on debut.
Debut colt by Triple Nine. Raced greenly in his June 18 trial to finish 8th. Stable is capable of preparing them first-up, but he looks like he will benefit from the experience.
First-starter by King Socks. Trailed home 9th in a wet track trial on June 4. Hard to recommend on that trial showing and will likely need the run.
Courvoisier colt making his debut. Showed some promise when 5th in a June 4 trial on a wet track. Could sneak into the minor money if he can overcome a slightly wider draw.
To Honor And Serve colt who showed some early speed to finish 3rd in his June 18 trial. Has some upside but faces a tough task from barrier 7 against experienced speed.
Hansen colt who was well beaten in his July 9 trial, finishing 10th. Drawn wide and looks up against it on debut.
Musket Man colt who caught the eye with a solid 4th in his June 25 trial. Despite the wide draw, he has the pedigree to handle the deep sand kickback and is a major player for the exotic slots.
